key_points: Time measurement allows precise calculation of motion and speed
Motion can be analyzed by studying distance travelled over time
Speed represents the rate at which distance is covered
Objects moving at constant speed exhibit uniform motion
Graphs and calculations can help compare different motions
examples: A car travelling 150 km in 3 hours has a speed of 50 km/h
A runner covering 400 m in 50 seconds has a speed of 8 m/s
Two vehicles travelling the same distance in different times will have different speeds
Uniform motion example: A train travelling 60 km every hour
exam_tips: Carefully analyze word problems involving distance, speed and time
Ensure units are consistent before applying formulas
Check calculations to avoid arithmetic mistakes
